name: Triple Layer Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 4 oz Swiss milk chocolate - finely chopped
4 oz Swiss white chocolate - finely chopped
4 oz Swiss dark chocolate - finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Wrap the bottom of a 10 x 3 inch springform pan with foil.
2. Generously butter the bottom and sides of the pan.
3. Crush 1/4 cup almonds in your hand and press onto the bottom of the pan. Refrigerate.
4. Melt chocolates separately.
5. Beat the cheese for 3-5 minutes until smooth and creamy. Add sugar, then beat in eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in cream until well blended.
6. Measure out approximately 2-1/3 cups of cheesecake batter and fold in milk chocolate just until combined. Pour into the prepared pan.
7. Divide the remaining batter in half. Add white chocolate to half and dark chocolate to the other. Gently pour the white mixture on top of the milk chocolate layer, then gently pour the dark chocolate layer on top.
8. Place the pan in a roasting pan on the middle rack of a 325 degree F oven. Pour boiling water into the roaster halfway up the springform pan. Bake for 1 hour and 45 minutes to 2 hours until set. Turn off the oven and let the cheesecake sit with the door closed for another hour. Remove from the oven and cool to room temperature. Refrigerate at least 5 hours.
9. Bring cream to a boil. Pour over chocolate and let sit for 30 seconds until chocolate melts. Whisk smooth. Pour glaze over the top of the cake and use a small spatula to spread evenly. Refrigerate 10 minutes to set the glaze.
10. Remove the cake from the pan and press remaining almonds around the sides.
